primaryStudies.citation: Kim SH, et al. ERR agonists potently reduce muscle fatigue and enhance endurance capacity through PGC-1alpha activation. J Med Chem. 2023;66(14):9497-9509.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: SLU-PP-332 enhanced endurance and reduced muscle fatigue in mice; mechanism involves ERR activation and PGC-1alpha signaling; demonstrates exercise mimetic properties

primaryStudies.citation: Willy PJ, Murray IR, Qian J, et al. Regulation of PPARgamma coactivator 1alpha (PGC-1alpha) signaling by an estrogen-related receptor alpha (ERRalpha) ligand. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA. 2004;101(24):8912-8917.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Foundational study on ERR-PGC-1alpha pathway; demonstrates ERR ligands can modulate metabolic gene expression

primaryStudies.citation: Narkar VA, et al. AMPK and PPARdelta agonists are exercise mimetics. Cell. 2008;134(3):405-415.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Seminal paper establishing concept of exercise mimetics; demonstrated pharmacological activation of exercise pathways; foundation for SLU-PP-332 development

primaryStudies.citation: Rangwala SM, et al. Estrogen-related receptor gamma is a key regulator of muscle mitochondrial activity and oxidative capacity. J Biol Chem. 2010;285(29):22619-22629.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: ERRgamma regulates mitochondrial function and oxidative metabolism in skeletal muscle; establishes ERR as metabolic target

primaryStudies.citation: Burris TP, et al. Nuclear receptors and their selective pharmacologic modulators. Pharmacol Rev. 2013;65(2):710-778.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of nuclear receptor pharmacology including ERR family; discusses potential of ERR modulators for metabolic disease

primaryStudies.citation: Giguere V. Transcriptional control of energy homeostasis by the estrogen-related receptors. Endocr Rev. 2008;29(6):677-696.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Establishes ERRs as master regulators of energy metabolism; details ERR-PGC-1alpha axis controlling oxidative metabolism

primaryStudies.citation: Fan W, et al. PPARs and ERRs: molecular mediators of mitochondrial metabolism. Curr Opin Cell Biol. 2018;33:49-54.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Reviews role of ERRs in mitochondrial biogenesis and metabolic adaptation; discusses potential therapeutic applications

primaryStudies.citation: Vernier M, et al. Estrogen-related receptors are targetable ROS sensors. Genes Dev. 2020;34(7-8):544-559.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: ERRs function as sensors for reactive oxygen species; links ERR signaling to antioxidant responses during exercise

evidenceSummary.regulatoryStatus: Not approved anywhere; research compound only; not submitted for clinical trials
evidenceSummary.keyLimitation: All evidence from preclinical studies in mice; no human pharmacokinetic, safety, or efficacy data exists

researchInstitutions.name: Saint Louis University
researchInstitutions.contribution: Primary development of SLU-PP-332; ERR agonist discovery program

researchInstitutions.name: University of Florida
researchInstitutions.contribution: Collaborative research on ERR biology and metabolic effects
